# Analysis of COMP110 Student Experience
## By: Jacob Shaffer

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
## Introduction
In this project, I analyzed survey data from COMP110 students to understand how prior programming experience affects their journey through the course.

<img src="./static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo. "  width="500"/>

## Visualization 1: Difficulty Distribution
<img src="./static/imgs/difficulty_boxplot.png" alt="Image of Boxplot to see if students with less experience find the course significantly harder."  width="500"/>

## Visualization 1: Student Backgrounds
<img src="./static/imgs/exp_distribution.png" alt="This initial count plot shows the distribution of prior experience in the class."  width="500"/>

## Visualization 3: Mastery Trends
<img src="./static/imgs/mastery_point_plot.png" alt="Image of Point Plot that tracks the average Mastery level reported by students across different academic years."  width="500"/>

## Conclusion
The survey results reveal a significant disparity in how students perceive course difficulty based on their background. While COMP110 is designed as an introductory course, students with no prior experience rated the difficulty at a mean of 5.5/7, whereas students with over a year of experience rated it significantly lower at 3.2/7. This "experience gap" is clearly visualized in the Difficulty Box Plot, where the median difficulty for beginners is notably higher than for their experienced peers.
